items.title: Secretary-General of ASEAN delivers remarks at the 23rd Shangri-La Dialogue
items.summary: Secretary-General of ASEAN, Dr. Kao Kim Hourn, today participated in the 23rd Shangri-La Dialogue, in Singapore, where he delivered remarks at the Second Plenary Session on “Priorities within Asia’s Shifting Strategic Landscape.” Dr. Kao highlighted ASEAN’s commitment to upholding ASEAN Cen The post Secretary-General of ASEAN delivers remarks at the 23rd Shangri-La Dialogue appeared first on ASEAN Main Portal .

items.title: Policy paper: AUKUS Defence Ministerial Joint Statement: 30 May 2026
items.summary: Australia, UK, and the US reaffirm shared commitment to delivering the AUKUS partnership and announce inaugural AUKUS signature project.

items.title: Hegseth Outlines U.S. Vision For Indo-Pacific at Annual Asia Defense Summit
items.summary: Secretary of War Pete Hegseth discussed the strategic vision for the Indo-Pacific region, as it relates to the new National Defense Strategy, during remarks at the annual International Institute for Strategic Studies Shangri-La Dialogue 2026 in Singapore.

items.title: The race for Madagascar has already started
items.summary: Often overlooked by Western policymakers, Madagascar holds major critical mineral reserves and sits astride key maritime routes. As Russia deepens ties, the United States can’t afford to cede strategic ground. The post The race for Madagascar has already started appeared first on Atlantic Council .

items.title: Report of the Head of OSCE Mission in Kosovo: UK statement, May 2026
items.summary: The UK thanks Ambassador McGurk for his concise and focused report presentation, and underlines support for the Mission's work on democratic processes and with non-majority communities.

items.title: Promote the antidote: Reducing the risk from toxins
items.summary: The policymaking community is not fully aware of the expansion of the threat from toxins. In this report, the Bipartisan Commission on Biodefense at Atlantic Council outlines the threat and how to negate it. items.title: Kroenig quoted in The Guardian on Rubio’s legacy
items.summary: On May 22, Atlantic Council vice president and Scowcroft Center senior director Matthew Kroenig was quoted in The Guardian, explaining that Trump's current foreign policy is not unlike that Rubio promoted on his 2016 presidential campaign. The post Kroenig quoted in The Guardian on Rubio’s legacy appeared first on Atlantic Council .

items.title: The Nordic-Baltic Eight policy brief: Geo-economic resilience
items.summary: New publication in the NB8 policy brief series by Thorsteinn Kristinsson (IIA) and Ludvig Broomé (UI) Geo-economic competition and the coercive use of economic statecraft are supplanting the rules-based system of liberal economic exchange. Small economies like the NB8 states are vulnerable to this development, including the potential weaponisation of external dependencies against them. This […]

items.title: Top Special Ops Civilian Recounts Successes, Lays Out Vision for Future
items.summary: Derrick Anderson, assistant secretary of war for special operations and low-intensity conflict, closed out the Special Operations Forces Week 2026 convention by underscoring the SOF community's recent successes and articulating his plan to prioritize five areas.

items.title: The Nordic-Baltic Eight policy brief: The circles of security
items.summary: Russia’s war against Ukraine has profoundly worsened Europe’s security environment, but it has also spurred deeper regional cooperation. The challenge now is to ensure that this growing number of initiatives remains purposeful, adds concrete value, and strengthens both the EU and NATO.

items.title: The Nordic-Baltic Eight policy brief: How to implement a full maritime services ban on Russian oil
items.summary: The EU is considering a full maritime services ban on Russian oil. While the final proposal is still under negotiation, the measure would replace the current oil price-cap regime by prohibiting European and allied companies from providing maritime services for Russian oil exports, regardless of the sale price. The ban would cover transportation, insurance, financing, […]

items.title: FIIA welcomes invitations to public events
items.summary: To mark the Finnish Institute of International Affairs’ 65th anniversary, FIIA researchers will be taking part in public events across Finland to discuss the most important current issues in foreign and security policy. We welcome invitations to a wide range of events and venues where a discussion on foreign policy would be a good fit […]

items.title: Study: Russia’s influence activities exploited special characteristics of bilateral relationship with Finland
items.summary: A study carried out by the Finnish Institute of International Affairs together with the University of Helsinki and the University of Tampere examined Russia’s full-spectrum influence activities directed at Finland since the 2000s and their effects on Finnish society and decision-making. The government-funded research project is based on the Government Programme and a description of […]

items.title: President of the Republic of Finland awards Juha Jokela the honour of Knight of the Order of the White Rose of Finland
items.summary: President of the Republic of Finland Alexander Stubb has decided to award Juha Jokela, Programme Director at FIIA, the honour of Knight of the Order of the White Rose of Finland. Jokela directs The European Union and Strategic Competition research programme. Each year on Finland’s independent day, the decorations of the Orders of the White Rose of […]
